summary: 2026-06-22 16:22:32 PDT — Scope and prohibited-assurance checks pass: no application logic or identifiers changed, and all SOC 2/ISO 27001/penetration-testing/DAST mentions are negative. However, several security documents make claims contradicted by the code or infrastructure, so the branch should not merge as written.

findings.title: False no-processors claim
findings.claim: The README and architecture SVG claim there are no third-party data processors, while this change identifies Railway as the host and encrypted-storage provider and the security policy explicitly lists GitHub, Railway, and Zoom as sub-processors. This is internally contradictory and materially misstates data handling.
findings.suggestion: State that Railway, GitHub, and Zoom are the identified providers/sub-processors, with no additional processors, analytics, advertising, or sale of data. Align README, SVG, public page, and policies.

findings.title: Key-rotation guidance risks data loss
findings.claim: The policy recommends rotating RATE_STORE_KEY as a standard containment step. The store derives every encryption key directly from that value, records no key version, and returns null when old files cannot decrypt. Rotation therefore silently makes every stored configuration unreadable.
findings.suggestion: Document a re-encryption migration using both old and new keys, or explicitly state that RATE_STORE_KEY rotation resets all stored configurations and requires user notification. Distinguish it from independently rotatable Zoom credentials.

findings.title: Logs are not scrubbed as claimed
findings.claim: The policy says operational logs are scrubbed of secrets and per-person values, but `/api/log` writes the submitted JSON body verbatim. The diagnostics path also submits raw Zoom SDK results, including participant and user-context data. No server-side redaction or field allowlist exists.
findings.suggestion: Either enforce a strict server-side logging schema with redaction and remove raw diagnostic payloads, or accurately document the personal data logs may contain, its sensitivity, retention, and access controls.

findings.title: GitHub test CI claim is false
findings.claim: The policy says GitHub runs CI for both CodeQL and tests, but the only workflow is CodeQL and it never runs `npm test` or `npm run build`. The described ruleset also requires only CodeQL, so tests are not a GitHub-enforced merge gate.
findings.suggestion: Add a test/build workflow and require its check, or revise every affected document to describe tests as a manually enforced pre-merge procedure rather than GitHub CI or a server-enforced gate.

findings.title: Dependabot jobs split a root workspace lockfile
findings.claim: This repository has one npm workspace rooted at `/` and one lockfile at `/package-lock.json`. Separate `/client` and `/server` jobs target directories without lockfiles and overlap the root workspace job, risking duplicate or manifest-only updates that do not maintain the committed lockfile. Dependabot scopes each entry by its configured manifest directory ([GitHub options reference](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/dependabot/working-with-dependabot/dependabot-options-reference)).
findings.suggestion: Use one npm update entry for `/`, which owns the workspace manifests and root lockfile, plus the separate GitHub Actions entry.
